# safariViewController(_:didCompleteInitialLoad:)

**Instance Method**

Tells the delegate that the initial URL load completed.

## Declaration

```swift
optional func safariViewController(_ controller: SFSafariViewController, didCompleteInitialLoad didLoadSuccessfully: Bool)
```

## Parameters

- **controller**: The view controller.
- **didLoadSuccessfully**: [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] if loading completed successfully; otherwise, [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false].

## Discussion

This method is invoked when [doc://com.apple.safariservices/documentation/SafariServices/SFSafariViewController] completes the loading of the URL that you pass to its initializer. The method is not invoked for any subsequent page loads in the same [doc://com.apple.safariservices/documentation/SafariServices/SFSafariViewController] instance.

## Working with the View Controller

- **safariViewController(_:activityItemsFor:title:)**: Tells the delegate that the user tapped an Action button.
- **safariViewControllerDidFinish(_:)**: Tells the delegate that the user dismissed the view.
- **safariViewController(_:excludedActivityTypesFor:title:)**
